name: Les Brown
abstract: Lester Raymond Brown (March 14, 1912 – January 4, 2001) was an American jazz musician who for over six decades (1938–2000) led his big band, later called Les Brown and His Band of Renown.

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Espinosa
about_quotes.evidence: Les called me and said, “Let’s go to the bar.” ... “Hal, I need a first trumpet player ... I’d like you to be my first chair player ... So I was ecstatic. I said, “You’ve got me Les, I’m ready.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Espinosa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 20, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Mandel
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first jazz vocals that I really remember... was Doris Day singing “Sentimental Journey” with Les Brown. And I really liked that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Mandel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Abe Most
about_quotes.evidence: I went with Les Brown at the end of 19, almost 20... I made 'Mexican Hat Dance,' it was my first recording with Les... Doris Day was on the band, the first band I was on with Les.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abe Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: I joined Les Brown and went the Rich’s Garden was the first gig
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: I had a great time with Lester’s band and played on every tune
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Frank "Machito" Grillo
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Les Brown recorded 'Montuno Clipper' and asks if he 'ever call you or tap you for ideas'; Interviewee responds 'No' and discusses general American curiosity, implying no direct professional interaction or personal connection.
